title: carlina sicula var. longibracteata
intro: Carlina sicula var. longibracteata, also known as the Sicilian carline thistle, is a plant species within the Carlina genus, native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and inflammation, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Carlina sicula var. longibracteata is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used in herbal remedies, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential side effects.
